prep.summary: You and Jonathan are deep into two parallel tracks — Project Genie (the AI-driven Jira/acceptance criteria automation tool) and Architecture as Code (a three-tier business/architecture/specification data model). As of 5/14, the Genie architecture is solid but needs GenAI Council queue entry for governance cover, and the team is mid-transition from Markdown to YAML-based data management; the AaC framework hit a schema-stability milestone in late April but is still waiting on stakeholder alignment with Osterkamp and McCarty to move from blueprint to MVP. Outstanding threads include the Technical Impact Analysis expansion for Genie, the TDSR lightweight app pilot with Cody, and the 'Architecture Recommendations' gap Jonathan flagged on 5/11.
tasks: Confirm whether Jonathan has submitted Project Genie to the GenAI Council queue and what the current queue status is.
Get a status update on the YAML/taxonomy.yaml migration — specifically whether HTML generation from YAML is now live or still pending.
Ask Jonathan where the Osterkamp and McCarty alignment meetings stand — these were flagged as blockers for moving AaC to MVP phase.
Follow up on the 'Architecture Recommendations' initiative Jonathan raised on 5/11 — determine if there is a concrete proposal or owner assigned.
Clarify the next step on the TDSR lightweight app pilot with Cody — whether specs are finalized and if Jonathan Stewart's review is complete.
prep.title: Meeting with Jonathan

focus_html:
Focus · In session
2:30 PM — 3:30 PM

Meeting with Jonathan

Prep

You and Jonathan are deep into two parallel tracks — Project Genie (the AI-driven Jira/acceptance criteria automation tool) and Architecture as Code (a three-tier business/architecture/specification data model). As of 5/14, the Genie architecture is solid but needs GenAI Council queue entry for governance cover, and the team is mid-transition from Markdown to YAML-based data management; the AaC framework hit a schema-stability milestone in late April but is still waiting on stakeholder alignment with Osterkamp and McCarty to move from blueprint to MVP. Outstanding threads include the Technical Impact Analysis expansion for Genie, the TDSR lightweight app pilot with Cody, and the 'Architecture Recommendations' gap Jonathan flagged on 5/11.

  • Confirm whether Jonathan has submitted Project Genie to the GenAI Council queue and what the current queue status is.
  • Get a status update on the YAML/taxonomy.yaml migration — specifically whether HTML generation from YAML is now live or still pending.
  • Ask Jonathan where the Osterkamp and McCarty alignment meetings stand — these were flagged as blockers for moving AaC to MVP phase.
  • Follow up on the 'Architecture Recommendations' initiative Jonathan raised on 5/11 — determine if there is a concrete proposal or owner assigned.
  • Clarify the next step on the TDSR lightweight app pilot with Cody — whether specs are finalized and if Jonathan Stewart's review is complete.
schedule_html:
2:30 PM
— 3:30 PM
Meeting with Jonathan
openwork_html: Mark's list Reach out to the first Paver guy for more information Due today
